steps.title: Welcome to Week 2
steps.body: You survived Week 1. Now you know the basics: clear prompts, useful answers, and when to trust them. Week 2 is about building reusable tools and workflows so AI works for you without starting from scratch every time.

steps.title: The Power of Templates
steps.body: Most people ask AI the same kinds of questions over and over. The smartest thing you can do is save a template. One good prompt template can save you hours every week.

steps.title: A reusable template
steps.prompt: I need to write a [type of message] to [audience]. The key point I want to communicate is: [main point]. The tone should be [tone]. Here is the context: [paste relevant details]. Please draft the message and suggest two alternative versions.

steps.title: Why templates work
steps.question: What makes a prompt template better than writing a fresh prompt each time?
options: They save time by reusing what works
You should never change a template
AI refuses to answer without a template
feedback: Even good templates need tweaking, but they save you the hard part.
Exactly. The thinking is done — you just fill in the blanks.
Templates can and should be adjusted as you learn.
AI works fine without one, but templates make you faster.

steps.title: Your life, templated
steps.body: Think about what you asked AI this week. Was it writing an email? Planning meals? Summarizing an article? Drafting a reply? Whatever it was, that is a template waiting to happen.

steps.title: Build your first template

fields.label: What situation do you use AI for most?
What parts stay the same every time?
fields.placeholder: my name, my role, the tone I use

steps.template: I regularly need help with {{situation}}. The parts that never change: {{reusable_parts}}. The parts that change each time: {{variable_parts}}. Create a fill-in-the-blank template I can reuse.

steps.title: Paste the template AI gave you
steps.body: Paste the template AI created for you. You will use it tomorrow.

steps.title: Beyond text — your AI console
steps.body: Chat interfaces are fine, but the real unlock is building your own console of AI tools. A 'console' just means having your go-to AI apps and sites ready in one place — like bookmarks for different problems.

steps.title: Your starter console
steps.body: Pick your main AI chat tool (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Grok). Add bookmarks for: a writing tool, an image generator, a search tool. That is your console. You do not need more than that.

questions.label: Which AI tool will be your main one?
What other AI tools (image, search, writing) would you add to your console?

steps.title: Good fit for a template?
steps.question: Write a single email to my boss.
feedback: Yes. Writing is one of the best uses of AI prompting.
Exactly. Emails are perfect for templates.

steps.title: Good fit for a template?
steps.question: Tell me what I should have for breakfast.
feedback: AI can suggest ideas, but this is a personal choice, not a task.
Right. AI can help plan meals, but decide what to eat yourself.

questions.question: What is the main benefit of prompt templates?
options: You never need to change them
They save time by reusing what works

questions.question: You should keep all your AI tools as separate browser tabs with no organization.
A set of go-to AI tools arranged by use case is called an AI ______.

steps.title: Day 8 complete — Template Builder
steps.body: You built your first reusable template and designed your AI console. Tomorrow you will learn how to chain tools together into real workflows.
